greetingDefault: Buying, selling, or just looking? Looking's free. Everything else costs.
greetingFriendly: Back again! Good. I've got something you'll want to see.
greetingHostile: You've got nerve coming back here. What do you want?
farewellDefault: Come back when your pockets are fuller.
unknownKeywordResponse: Never heard of it. Which might mean I have, and I'm being careful.

keywords.response: Yara. Just Yara. Names are assets — I don't give mine away.
I deal in curiosities. Things that find their way to me by various routes and find their way to new owners by various other routes. I ask few questions. That's worth paying for.
Depends what you need. I've got salvage from the wilderness, items of uncertain Church legality, information, and a very nice alien artefact I'd sell at a loss just to stop having to store it.

Information costs more than goods, because goods can be replaced. Word is — and this costs you two firebirds — the serfs going missing near the old ruins aren't the first. There were disappearances fifteen years ago too. Different Baron, same ruins.
Blue lamp is lit, you say? Ha. Orlan thinks he keeps his distance from me. He doesn't. But I keep his distance for him, which amounts to the same thing. What does he need moved?

Ur-Obun piece. Found near the old stone circle. Hums faintly, which I find unsettling. The Church would confiscate it and call it heresy. A Psi-sensitive might find it... useful. Fifty firebirds and it's yours.

The old stone circle in the wilderness? I'd avoid it at night if I were you. My supplier stopped coming through there last week. The one before him too, actually. I've started using a different route.
Big word. I prefer 'informal economy.' The city has one. Every city does. I am a small part of it. The part that doesn't hurt people — that's important to me.
Come back when your pockets are fuller. Or when you've found something interesting.
